reviews.review_text:
This game is so good. I can proudly say I finished it with all achievements—thanks to my friend who helped me out.

It Takes Two really gives you a different kind of gaming experience; you do so many things together. Following May and Cody’s story made me realize how decisions can shape someone’s way of thinking (you’ll get it when you play). I’m happy with the ending, [spoiler] but I still ask for justice for the elephant :< I don’t think the queen deserved that. [/spoiler] This game really earned its GOTY title.

Great gameplay. Abhorrent story and characters.
Played through the story once, and did not want to replay it. The gameplay in the game is very enjoyable (pretty much all of it) and constantly varies to offer a new experience. But I was completely turned off by the story and its characters.
The characters are one dimensional and quite unlikable. They constantly bicker with eachother (not even argueing, just bickering) about everything and anything. Their dicision making is fully ego-centric and at times deeply disturbing. The story can at parts best be described as psychological horror. Since you cannot make any dicisions in this game, you have to play out the abhorrent story or turn the game off.

The game itself is fun to play. But the story itself is so terrible that I cannot in good conscience recommend it.
If however you ignore the story completely the game itself it very playable! So that is what I'm doing for my second playthrough. Swap the spoken language to Japanese (or any other language you are not proficient in), disable the subtitles, and skip all the cut-scenes. This way you can make your own story and make up reasons why the characters do what they do. Way more fun to play this way!
I played this game with my gf, it was so much fun and loved every second of it (cuz of her ofc). amazing game:)

I am playing this game with a friend who don't know how to play games. Most people must be get mad about it but i love how she can't play and struggling. If u will play this game with your friend or your boy/girlfriend, i'm sure you'll gave a lot of fun.

Haven’t finished it yet but I’ve already played it with 3 different buddies and loved every run. The puzzles are super creative and every chapter feels fresh. Easily one of the most fun co-op games I’ve played.
I just wish there was a solo mode too.. The world and puzzles are soOo good that I really wanna replay it alone sometimes without needing someone else..
Hazelight please drop "It Takes one" 🙏🏻
Fun and creative experience, albeit a bit too long for my liking.

The game is very nice and very fun to play with your partner. It creates quite a few moments of conflict, tension and frustration when you're trying to do all these somewhat challenging things with a person who doesn't play nearly the same amount of games as you, but it's also so very satisfying when they succeed.

The story is cute, whimsical and enjoyable, all the way until for no valid reason whatsoever you're both required to become absolutely despicable monsters. This part is the only reason I'm giving the game a thumbs down - it feels out of left field, makes no sense and just feels unnecessarily meanspirited.

Either way if you can stomach some Geneva Convention-breaking war crimes and live with yourself it's an absolutely masterfully made game. Total blast.
Absolutely stunning visuals, it is entertaining with a skilled gamer.

Only just talking about this game, I think it's not too good,not as amazing as everyone say. some part und level were creative and fun, but overall it felt just pretty good to me. just not boring.
But I think the truly sets apart is its collaboration model, two people play  on different screen, but you can see each other,and each controll their own. character,and solving things together.
But honestly, after finishing, in my mind wasn’t really the levels or the gameplay. it's the person who plays  this game with you
Because it was a game I played with my girlfriend, when I looks back afterwards, i was really really really angry to myself.
I realized I’m more impatient than I thought. I get annoyed easily, especially when we get stuck somewhere or keep failing the same part. Sometimes I react too fast before thinking. sometimes I say things with emotion without meaning to sound harsh. and sometimes I blame her for things that aren’t even her fault.
At that moment I don’t  notice it. so  looking back now, I truly feel like a shameless scoundrel.
A lot of the time, she was the one being patient with me and she kept apologizing to me ,but I had absolutely no patience for her and keep blaming her.
Even sometimes I made the mistake, but she would still apologize first. Now, whenever I think back about that , I'll always feeling bad 
Because there were moments where I could’ve been softer, more patient ,but instead I let impatience and anger come out first.
This game feels funny in that way. It looks like a co-op puzzle game on the surface, but when you actually play it with someone close to you, it feels like it shows more than that:you  can see how you work together.you can see how each person reacts under pressure.who gets impatient first.who says sorry first.who always treats you gently.
These things are hard to notice in everyday life, but when playing games with loved ones, people really do hurt her who loves you best without any scruples. So I think, I'm a really really really really ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ bastard.
I also clearly realize that sometimes the way I talk to her isn't good, sometimes I joke toomuch. Sometimes I say things too casually, sometimes I say something a little negative or put her down without really thinking about it, at that time it feels small, like “it’s nothing,” just something said in the moment. But now, looking back carefully, I realize that this was really bad, because the sharpness of language never lies in the speaker's choice of words and tone, nor in its specific environment and background, but rather in the listener's feelings.
I really shouldn't have treated her like that before.Because the truth is, she’s really been good to me.
So, when dealing with someone close to you, if you feel very angry with her, try slowing down and looking at her. She's actually been following you all along. She might not be a big gamer, but for you, she's willing to be scolded and patiently stay by your side. You absolutely must cherish her.

You guys NEED to remove that elephant section. Also the main characters might be the DUMBEST people on planet earth.

They need to make their kid happy, so what do they do? 

Whatever wholesome thoughts you just had, throw them out the window because the answer was to brutally rip apart her favorite toy limb by limb. The animal is still alive and screaming throughout all of this by the way. This is when I stopped playing the game. 

Can you guess how this worked out? Poorly. Who would've thought. The situation became worse because it turns out destroying a child's favorite toy doesn't make them very happy.

This is the kind of game where your experience depends on who you play with. I didn’t even expect it to last over 10 hours. It has a lot of mechanics, secrets, mini-games, beautiful visuals, and some dark humor that fits surprisingly well.

10/10 if you play with a friend or partner. It really changes the whole experience.

An absolute blast to play. The puzzles and gameplay really depend on the cooperation and communication with your partner and were a great bonding experience. The story was very cozy and "Disney-esque" but there are some parts in the story that will leave you speechless... iykyk. Great for long distance partners via online play and probably better in couch co-op. Strongly recommended!

Awesome experience, to play it with a friend, family member or partner.

Very straightforward, and even kids can play it

This is the first game my non-gamer wife finished with me.

It is adorable, not too hard and just really a lot of stuff to do and fool around with.

10/10!

Through out this game it truly took two. also we did not argue or have any issues. This game was very cute and extremely fun to play with someone. Had a blast and loved it all the secret achievements were super funny
Excelent game, very emotional and full of joy, me and my wife love the game

It is one of the best games I've played if not the best game of this kind. I liked everything about it and I strongly recommend to play on a controller because it gets more immersive. Overall I will give an 12/10 for this. The visuals are it's strong point for sure and the way you can find minigames and explore to some extent is very nice. Not one single time did I find the game to be repetitive or boring. It gives you all kind of perspectives from 3D to 2D. The only downside I could find is that I can't play this game for the first time again:)))) Looking forward to games such as this because I'm not sure if I can find another one as good as this game is.
Played through half of the game by myself with two controllers, because my friends can't be bothered to install it, but a fun time when I was with them locally, and a great challenge outside of that.

My wife an i have played about 80% of the game. As a long time mmo and gaming enjoyer i can't believe the incredible boss fights and genuinely some of the most incredible landscapes I have ever encountered in video games. Its 2026 now, I finally understand why this game is so high up on peoples lists.
This was an amazing game to play with my long distance partner, it was never too hard but each mechanic was super fun and entertaining
Too simple puzzles for people who play games regularly but too tedious for newbies. Quite difficult to duo with a new player reasonably. Art style is nice enough.

The best game ever! 12 out of 10, despite some aesthetic issues. This was the first video game my wife and I completed together. It's a meaningful, enjoyable, educational, and lovely experience. We really appreciate the development team.
Awesome game. Fun gameplay and good storry.

Game has a very good story line. It's also helped me and my wifes relationship with our communication. I would recommend it to any struggling couple. Has lots of puzzles and little side games. I highly recommend.
Excellent game, great level design and story
This was both beautiful and heartbreaking. The story-telling and world-building the game offered has me absolutely stunned.
cute story, nice collab game. worth playing a second time for playing the other partners role. easy acheivements.

I loved this game. It's truly co-op in the sense that you must work together to progress, instead of just "playing next to each other"
One of the best games for two people. It is fun for friends and couples.
Most beautiful game i have played, the best part I played it with my little girl

It Takes Two is easily one of the most creative co-op games I’ve ever played.

Every level introduces something completely new, so the gameplay never gets repetitive. One minute you’re solving puzzles, the next you’re flying, fighting, racing, or doing something totally unexpected. The game constantly keeps both players involved.

What makes it special is how perfectly it’s designed for co-op. Communication and teamwork actually matter, and it becomes way more fun with someone you can laugh and argue with along the way.

The visuals, music, and level design are all fantastic, but the real highlight is how much fun the game is from start to finish.

A masterpiece in co-op gaming.

10/10 — play it with a friend, partner, or sibling for the best experience.
Great game overall, great story and gameplay.
Very fun game however when the 2 characters fight, the players also fight questioning their relationship

It Takes Two is one of the most fun games I have ever played.

The story isn't trying to be some Oscar-worthy drama, but it is charming, funny, and full of heart. The characters are memorable, the dialogue is entertaining, and while some parts require you to ignore the occasional questionable logic, the game never takes itself too seriously. It knows exactly what kind of experience it wants to be.

What truly makes It Takes Two special is its incredible variety. Every chapter feels like an entirely new game. Just when you think you've seen everything the game has to offer, it introduces a completely new mechanic, gameplay style, or setting.

One moment you're surviving dangerous environments together, the next you're fighting giant wasps and bees, exploring a magical snow globe, controlling toy-like contraptions, battling through a space-themed adventure, or taking on the roles of a mage and a warrior in a fantasy-inspired world. The game constantly throws new ideas at you and somehow manages to make almost all of them work. It never felt repetitive for a single moment.

The controls are excellent and movement feels smooth, responsive, and satisfying throughout the entire adventure. I was actually worried that the camera movement and constant perspective changes might trigger my motion sickness, but surprisingly, the opposite happened. Everything felt natural and refreshing, and I often found myself playing for hours without any discomfort.

The co-op design is also outstanding. Every mechanic is built around teamwork, communication, and cooperation. Both players always have something meaningful to do, and many of the game's best moments come from figuring things out together.

The visuals are beautiful, the level design is incredibly creative, and the game is packed with small details, mini-games, and memorable moments that make every chapter feel unique.

I absolutely loved this game. It is one of those rare experiences where I was constantly excited to see what the next chapter would bring. If I had to describe It Takes Two in a single word, it would be fun. From beginning to end, it was one of the most enjoyable gaming experiences I have ever had.
peak game, played with my brother and we got together more ever since growing up

Never finished this game once because people kept leaving me.
Played it on PS4
Played it on Xbox
Played it on PC
Dammit.

this game is wonderful. there are so many fresh ideas and concepts that make each level at the vert least good and at the very best incredible. this is easily one of the most fun games ive ever palyed in my life. only problem was that stupid book

9/10

Easily the best game I’ve ever played. A massive display of artistry and creativity from start to finish. I don’t know what Mr. Fares is on, but he should definitely keep it up if it leads to masterpieces like this. I just wish it lasted longer, and I’m already looking forward to trying Split Fiction. 10/10
nice coop gameplay and story

probably wouldn't save my marriage but...
it will definitely be one of the best thing we did together

the story is well-tailored, the gameplay is intuitive and fun
even for someone almost never play any game before
character design, level design, voice acting all are done with quality in mind
and it runs perfectly smooth

enjoy it on both keyboard-mouse and joycon alike

10/10

best with your bestie :)

ABSOLUTLY best game to play with your partner!! or even your friend... or worst enemy maybe? maybe after this you guys made up xd

I had so much fun andf i need to play it again someday

what i think is a huge bonus point is that only one person of the two people playing the game has to own it. the other one can get it for free. or rather play with the person who bought it for free!!

story and gameplay were so good, the challanges were always new, didnt feel as if you just keep doing the same shht again and again! it was so wholesome designed the storyline was really good and funny aswell. best multiplayer
My wife and I have played this twice now. Each time we learn more about each other and get into amazing conversations. Couch Co-op games are the best, and this game is one of the best of them <3

Have just now finished the game at 14.7 hours, i would recommend the game.
Also gotta say playing this has made me hate and love the book of love.

*warning you may want to assassinate your friend or whoever your playing this with while playing*
Definitely recommend this game if you play with your friend who lives far away lol We got sucked into it and played for 6 hours straight it was awesome!

Wow what a much better game that I expected. Such a great pic and play random game with new and fresh mini styles and puzzles in each area. For sure much better than anticipated, buy on sale and you'll have no regrets.

1) Find true love
2) Get married
3) Regret everything and file for divorce
4) Play this game, find your love again, and reconsider your divorce
5) Get divorced anyway and marry whoever created this beautiful masterpiece
Very fun the multiplayer is impeckable and it never gets old.

It was one of the best surprises. I wasn't expecting to love it as much as I did. The story was so good. The book was hilarious.  I don't often write reviews. I felt like this one deserved one. If you are waffling to get it. I'd highly recommend to give it a shot.

This game is surprisingly awesome! The story, level design, puzzles, and character interaction are top-notch.  Many of the levels are trippy, artistic, and vibrant in a manner reminiscent of Little Big Planet.  I'm playing the game now with my 13 year old son and after a good session, we both gush about how amazing it is.  Money well spent!
Very fun and funny. Well designed, very easy but still engaging.
Seriously such an AMAZING game with such a great concept that all couples and best friends can play:D Cutie made me cry so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hard tho i hated that part

I played this with one of my friends on the PlayStation and he tortured me to a point I have never forgotten... Amazing game though! The name "It Takes Two" was completely thrown into a fire fueled by nuclear waste. as soon as he even looked at the controller for the PS4.
(could be in need of therapy)
10/10 me and my gf had fun, we felt and our string got tighter after playing this.

This game was so much fun from start to finish IMO.

The gameplay keeps you hooked because it gives you so much different things to have to do and accomplish, that's practically it. Plus it also has it's moments where you troll your duo and you start cackling.

The story is okay, not my cup of tea but the gameplay itself was enough to keep me playing and to finish it.
Playing through this with my daughter and it's fantastic fun! It's actually amazingly well designed, full of variety, colour and fun. Great experience.
A magnificent 2 player experience that really makes it feel like you are going to divorce your best friend when they ♥♥♥♥ up the same timed jump for the 12th time.
This is a great game, perfect for playing with friends or couples.it's highly replayable
tjis game will either make you kill the person your playing with or youll have a stronger relationship
i just loved this game except that octupus fight in the ocean i sucked at that part but this game is pure gem

It Takes Two is an amazing game, It also runs really well on steam

loved it

 5 stars
amazing laid back but still a good amount of challenging. perfect game to play with your SO. super optimized to, runs well on my gtx 1050m laptop

Fun to play, you can play it with anyone, played ot with my frens, no matter if it glit he's it's fun- or maybe it's just who you play it with, or maybe both. Yeah, both.

It's not hard or difficult, simple puzzles and a lot, lot, loooootttt of teamwork. The friend pass is a godsend too. Makes it much more easier to play.

Definitely recommend if ur looking for a chill game to play with ur bestie/lover/friend, as well as streaming it !
The best game I ever played, very detailed story and locations
this game is so fun to play with a friend! the puzzles are cool and unique. btw, be ready to jump alot lol
Great game to play with a significant other, assuming your relationship isn't rocky.
Wonderful game to play with your significant other. Recommend 10/10. Split fiction is next

It Takes Two is one of the most fun experiences I have ever had. Every level is creative with super fun and unique mechanics and a beautiful atmosphere. A fantastic story to pull at your heart strings to add to it makes it a must play for all!

It Takes Two is honestly one of the most creative and fun games i’ve played in a long time. Every chapter introduces something new, so the gameplay never really feels boring or repetitive. One minute you’re laughing at how ridiculous everything is, and the next minute you’re arguing because one of you missed a jump for the 15th time.

What I really liked about this game is how much it actually forces you to work together. You can’t just run off and do your own thing, communication and timing genuinely matter. There were moments where me and my boyfriend were LOCKED IN trying to figure out puzzles, and other moments where we were blaming each other like our lives depended on it lol.

The visuals are amazing, the environments are super creative, and the amount of detail put into every level honestly surprised me. The game constantly keeps things fresh. Also the emotional/story moments hit harder than i expected for a game about tiny doll parents.
My and my girlfriend love this game, we particularly appreciate the creativity and passion put on realizing this game, absolutely reccomended.
A great game for couples. Perfect for bonding. A bit hard on the GPU.
Just started my third playthrough, each with a different person. That's how much I love playing this game

The best game to play with your partner, featuring an excellent story and engaging gameplay. The puzzles aren’t overly difficult, which actually makes the experience even more enjoyable.

Disclaimer: This game truly requires two players and is best experienced with your life partner.
I love the story to this game! Really cute and fun with simple puzzles.

This game is sooo freaking fun I never thought I can find a game sooo worth it to play. The mc's personalities are so funny and I also love the lore. I thought it would just be a common platformer but I love the puzzles and how we can collaborate to play the game. It's also so optimized as well even with low end PCs. I don't have a graphics card only VRAM but it's also so optimized as well. 

I suck a platformers, but this one is a mix of both so I thoroughly enjoy them.
Great game with great story and characters. Perfect game for couples and awesome couch co-op experience.

TLDR: Having beaten this with both my GF (on the switch) and another time with a friend (PC), it's a good purchase with memorable experiences. It's also gonna make you laugh at them. Mini games range from wack a mole to chess, campagin locations range from inside a snowglobe to outer space, weapons changing from explodable goop to flying nails, the game delivers on variety and fun. Sometimes so much so that it feels like they're flinging sh!t at a wall to see what sticks, but... it strangely works.

Story: Cody and May are parents wanting a divorce. Ze child makes a wish. They turn into living dolls. Annoying a$$ stupid ♥♥♥♥!ng book of love tries to fix their relationship. This whole premise has been used many times before in one way or another in many a movies... But where it gets interesting is how a game manages to make you go through so many different places, vibes and games that'll make you adapt to a new thinking pattern every couple of hours that it makes it always fresh.

Gameplay: The brunt of the game. Trying to explain this would take way too long so I'll just go over what doesn't change every couple of minutes/hours.
You can dash and double jump. Yes, both.
Falling in a pit kills you.
Spamming a button brings you back to life faster.
Playing as either Cody or May is vastly different and people I've played with tend to think Cody's powers tend to be funner, so keep it in mind.
That's all you need to know for now.
As a d!ckhead book would say: ''Now go out there and fix your relationshiiiiiip... Vamonos!''

Graphics: While the ''real'' ingame world looks kinda bland and oddly empty, the ingame world you can interact with is chuckfull of details and things to interact with. And boy oh boy does it have range. From what one could consider a realistic playroom to an acid trip, it's all there.

Audio: While some dialogue can be a little cringy, the overall audio quality is just fine.
One of the best co-op games to ever exist! It was so much fun, the journey taking you through different challenges and beautiful landscapes.

Insuferable characters but brilliant level design and artistic direction. 

When you are playing this with your partner it will feel sometimes like you are mirroring the characters in the game which is honestly genius. 

The game is also very accessible to partners that have never played games before. It did take a while for my gf to get used to 3D controls but she got the hang of it after a few sessions. 

Things like like holding the jump button longer to jump higher, moving the control stick in mid air to position a landing we often take for granted but she had to learn all of these subtle movements from scratch! Which was fascinating. For us gamers the subtleties of 3D controls are so ingrained in us we never think about them consciously!

'It takes two' is one of the most creative and enjoyable co-op gamnes I've ever played. From the start to finish, the game constantly introduces fresh mechanics and fun challenges that keep the level exciting and unpredictable. The team work focused gameplay is incredibly well designed, making cooperation feel rewarding instead of frustrating. Overall, It Takes Two is a masterpiece of co-op gaming and a must play for anyone who enjoys playing with friends, family or a partner. It's fun, emotional. creative and easily one of the best multiplayer experiences available.

i thought co-op games were not my thing. clearly i was wrong because this was so much fun. i had never laughed this hard while playing a game. the puzzles were very interesting too. i'm still trying to 100% it so it's kinda frustrating when there's no skip button for cutscenes but 10/10 overall.
Visually, this game is an absolute flex. The graphics look straight-up like a high-budget Pixar movie and every new area is pure eye candy, especially the space and clockwork levels.

This game was fun to play and was pretty easy to understand for beginners, but the first boss fight was too hard for a beginner to pick up right away, you should watch videos on the boss fight first for any people new to gaming
very entertaining and challenging tasks as a pair. Also very nice story

The game is cute and I think the artstyle is fun. It definitely loses some of its luster as you play though. Feels very rollercoaster-y and fits for being a game you play with someone who doesn't play many games. The story itself was meh for my partner and I and we really did not like the characters, only the daughter. And we did NOT like the ending and thought it was a bit hamfisty to try and make the players feel something. But for a nice little chill game to relax and play together it definitely makes it there. Puzzles very easy to figure out and very streamlined to playthrough. Worst one was the spiders as it gave both of us headaches with all the spinning. Probably won't play through it again as the novelty of each areas mechanics isn't a strong enough pull and could not really care for the characters. Got this for like 10 bucks and it comes with the friend pass so for that, recommended. But overall maybe just a 5/10, ending did not feel good.
Creative, charming, and the ultimate test of cooperation, friendship and emotional damage. 10/10 — would yell “jump now” too late again

My SO and I finished this game and tested our relationship we enjoyed every mini game albeit steam only recognizing that we only played for 14 hrs. 

recommended to couples before marriage.

Me and my partner (she/her) play this game together and have lots of fun. I play May and am responsible for dps, puzzle-solving, parkour challenges, finding mini-games and strategising. She plays Toby and keeps her ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ mouth shut and does what i say.

We make a great team - 7.4/10
I recently played it with my bestie 🫶🏽 it  was an interesting experience, and definitely worth playing
Great game! lots of fun, and a unique story line - fun new game mechanics. Can't wait to see how the game ends. Only 3 hours in and highly recommend.
Brilliant and fantastic. What a game!

The game mechanics are fun, the mini games are great but the characters are insufferable(all of them including the book).  and there are a few parts of the game that makes them feel unredeemable. 

The beginning of the game was quite good with puzzles but the final 1/4 to 1/3 felt very repetitive (a lot of run away from thing / environment) 

Final story ending was meh. 

A lot of the more fun challenges seem to be for the female character with the male character more as a support. 

Spoiler:



The part that really sticks out of the characters being unredeemable was the joyful dismemberment of the elephant (child’s favorite stuffed animal) as it begs for its life. Really left me with an icky feeling and was kinda done with the game after that (although I did finish it).

This game was soo much fun, there of course were tense moments and frustrations... you need good communication, there were a few things I was thinking and expecting my partner to "just do" but didnt get the memo lol. 
Highly recommend giving this a go with a loved one or a good friend.
One of the best co-op games that i have played, with great visuals and great story.

It Takes Two is an amazing game with a very touching story. It has everything that a good story should have, from your introduction, to the climax, and then the conclusion. The level design alone is very creative, and I enjoyed the different mechanics May and Cody have for each level, and how one of them is reliant on the other to progress through the story. This is the most I have enjoyed playing a game in a long time. ESPECIALLY with the love of my life. Call this game whatever you'd like. Coupleslop, friendslop, but it is neither of those things. It is a work of art, a true masterpiece, and a diamond in the dirt of other modern games. I highly *highly* recommend this game to anyone who wants an intriguing, indulging, and healing experience with their significant other. 

10/10 Highly Recommended

Great game. Got it on sale, totally worth the price. Loved playing through with my girlfriend. Only took us about 14 hours but you can switch characters and do the whole thing again with puzzles unique to each character. Loved the hobgoblin Rose.
This is one of the most creative games I've ever played, lowk goated. The co-op is great fun to play with friends.

Amazing game for split screen on the projector. 
Recommended!

Me and my wife who doesn't play videogames at all decided to try the game out she is now interested in more co-op games to play together! I cant recommend this enough for any gamer and non-gamer couple since this game was a very fun experience.

Absolutely worth every penny. Had so much fun with my wife. Very well designed from the beginning to the end. 

Although at the end it felt like May wasn't that much in love even after everything happened.
